etymology.description: semantic 衣(衣) (yī, "clothing") + phonetic 尚(尚) (shàng) -> lower garment
etymology.interpretation: The character uses 衣(衣) (yī, "clothing") to indicate it is related to garments, while 尚(尚) (shàng) provides the phonetic cue. This combination refers specifically to lower garments like skirts or robes, emphasizing the notion of what is worn below the waist.

examples.chinese: 她穿着美丽的裙裳(她穿著美麗的裙裳)。
examples.english: She is wearing a beautiful skirt.

examples.chinese: 古代的礼服常有长长的裳(古代的禮服常有長長的裳)。
examples.pinyin: Gǔdài de lǐfú cháng yǒu chángcháng de cháng.
examples.english: Ancient ceremonial dresses often had long lower garments.

examples.chinese: 秋风吹起她的轻裳(秋風吹起她的輕裳)。
examples.pinyin: Qiūfēng chuī qǐ tā de qīngcháng.
examples.english: The autumn wind lifts her light lower garment.
